docs.title: PraisonAIBio
docs.text:
Open-source AI agents for systems biology — discover, simulate, and compare curated models from BioModels.org using PraisonAI.

For biologists and lab scientists. No heavy coding required.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
graph LR
    Q[Your question] --> A[AI agent]
    A --> B[(BioModels.org)]
    A --> R[Report or simulation]
  1. Ask a question in plain English.
  2. The agent searches BioModels.org.
  3. You get a shortlist, summary, or simulation preview.

docs.title: Try in 30 seconds
docs.text:
No AI (fastest) 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/small/01_search.py
With AI agent 
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/01_find_models.py
YAML workflow 
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text: Task Start here Install Install Copy-paste tasks Quick tasks Understand workflows For researchers See all tools Tools Run examples Examples

docs.title: Demo model
docs.text: BIOMD0000000206 — Teusink yeast glycolysis (used in cookbooks and benchmarks).

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub
  • BioModels.org
  • PraisonAI

docs.title: praisonai-bio
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
praisonai-bio init              # scaffold .praisonai
praisonai-bio validate check    # package + toolsets OK
praisonai-bio tools validate    # 21 entry points
praisonai-bio doctor mcp        # MCP + BASICO check

docs.title: PraisonAI wrapper
docs.text:
Correct patterns (not praisonai agent run):

praisonai run --agent biomodels-scout "Find glycolysis models"
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

docs.title: MCP servers
docs.text:
python mcp/sysbio-server/server.py       # 11 T2B tools
python mcp/biomodels-server/server.py    # read-only BioModels
praisonai-bio-mcp                        # console entry (if installed)

See MCP.

docs.title: Development
docs.text: For contributors and CI.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[dev,simulation]"

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
./scripts/test_all.sh

Or step by step:

pytest tests/unit -q
pytest tests/integration -q      # live BioModels.org
pytest tests/agentic -q          # needs OPENAI_API_KEY

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
python scripts/validate_repo.py
bash scripts/check_no_submission.sh
python scripts/generate_catalog.py

docs.title: Docs site
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve    # http://127.0.0.1:8000
mkdocs build    # output in site/

docs.title: Package layout
docs.text:
src/praisonai-bio/praisonai_bio/
  adapters/     # BioModels API, BASICO
  tools/        # @tool functions + entry points
  toolsets/     # prebuilt toolsets
  workflows/    # (YAML lives in repo workflows/)

See AGENTS.md on GitHub for contributor rules.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Copy, run, done.

docs.title: Before you run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

Agent examples also need:

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

docs.title: Small — no AI agent
docs.text:
One tool, a few lines, no API key.

Script What it does examples/small/01_search.py Search BioModels.org examples/small/02_model_info.py Metadata for one model examples/small/03_trust_score.py Curation trust score examples/small/04_validate_sbml.py Validate SBML structure 
python examples/small/01_search.py

docs.title: Big — AI agent
docs.text:
Script What it does examples/big/01_find_models.py Agent search examples/big/02_summarise_model.py Agent summary examples/big/03_discovery_study.py Search + rank + recommend examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py Full glycolysis walkthrough 
python examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py

docs.title: YAML — no Python
docs.text:
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

See Workflows and Quick tasks.

docs.title: Do I need to code?
docs.text: No for small examples and YAML workflows. Agent examples need an API key but not writing code.

docs.title: What is BIOMD0000000206?
docs.text: The demo model — Teusink yeast glycolysis. Used in cookbooks and docs.

docs.title: Why import praisonai_bio?
docs.text: Toolsets register at import time. YAML/CLI will not find sysbio-full without it.

docs.title: Simulation fails / BASICO missing
docs.text:
pip install "praisonai-bio[simulation]"

Discovery and search work without BASICO.

docs.title: BioModels vs EBI URLs?
docs.text: PraisonAIBio uses https://www.biomodels.org by default. Override with BIOMODELS_BASE_URL.

docs.title: How is this different from Talk2BioModels?
docs.text:
T2B focuses on simulation after you pick a model. PraisonAIBio adds discovery, ranking, workflows, and multi-agent orchestration.

Compare →

docs.title: How is this different from ClawBio?
docs.text:
ClawBio is genomics skills. PraisonAIBio is BioModels / SBML simulation.

Compare →

docs.title: For researchers
docs.text: PraisonAIBio helps you find, check, and simulate models from BioModels.org — without browsing thousands of entries by hand.

docs.title: The problem it solves
docs.text: Manual work With PraisonAIBio Weeks browsing BioModels Minutes: search + ranked shortlist Reading papers for assumptions Agent summary + trust score Setting up COPASI by hand Optional one-command simulation

docs.title: A — Direct tools (small examples)
docs.text:
You run a short script. No chat.

→ Best for: quick lookup, one model ID, validation.

→ Folder: examples/small/

docs.title: B — AI agent (big examples)
docs.text:
You ask in English. The agent picks tools.

→ Best for: open questions, comparing models, reports.

→ Folder: examples/big/

docs.title: Typical study flow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Research question] --> B[Search BioModels]
    B --> C[Rank & trust score]
    C --> D[Assumption review]
    D --> E{Happy?}
    E -->|Yes| F[Simulate]
    E -->|No| B
    F --> G[Compare & report]

YAML workflows in workflows/discovery/ automate this team.

Workflow file What it does biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml Question → shortlist biomodels_assumption_review.yaml Check assumptions (human approval step) biomodels_baseline_simulation.yaml Run baseline simulation biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml All phases together

Run any workflow:

praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

docs.title: Demo model to try
docs.text:
BIOMD0000000206 — yeast glycolysis (Teusink et al.)

Used in cookbooks, examples, and benchmarks.

docs.title: Need help choosing?
docs.text:
If you… Use Know the model ID examples/small/02_model_info.py Have a pathway name only examples/big/01_find_models.py Want a full pipeline workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

See Tools at a glance for the full tool list in plain language.

docs.title: Get started
docs.text: Three steps. No biology coding required.

docs.title: Step 1 — Install
docs.text:
cd PraisonAIBio
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

docs.title: Step 2 — Try a search (no AI)
docs.text:
python examples/small/01_search.py

You get: a list of curated models matching “glycolysis”.

docs.title: Step 3 — Ask an agent (optional)
docs.text:
Set your OpenAI key, then:

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/01_find_models.py

You get: a short answer with recommended model IDs.

docs.title: What next?
docs.text:
Goal Command Check one model python examples/small/02_model_info.py Trust / curation score python examples/small/03_trust_score.py Full glycolysis walkthrough python examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py YAML workflow (no Python) praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

Open Quick tasks for a task menu.

docs.title: From GitHub
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/MervinPraison/PraisonAIBio.git
cd PraisonAIBio
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

Required once per session

Always run import praisonai_bio before YAML or CLI toolsets.

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text:
Simulation (BASICO/COPASI) 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]"
Plotting 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[plot]"
Everything 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[all,dev]"

docs.title: PraisonAI CLI (workflows)
docs.text:
Install the PraisonAI wrapper for YAML workflows and agents:

pip install praisonai

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
praisonai-bio validate check
praisonai-bio tools validate

Expected: 21 tools, toolsets including sysbio-full.

docs.title: Environment
docs.text:
Variable Purpose OPENAI_API_KEY Agent examples (big/) BIOMODELS_BASE_URL Default https://www.biomodels.org

Next: Get started

docs.title: Quick tasks
docs.text: Pick a task. Copy the command. Run it.

docs.title: Task 1 — Find models
docs.text:
Question: “What glycolysis models exist?”

python examples/small/01_search.py

No API key needed.

docs.title: Task 2 — Inspect one model
docs.text:
Question: “What is BIOMD0000000206?”

python examples/small/02_model_info.py

Demo model: Teusink yeast glycolysis.

docs.title: Task 3 — Is this model trustworthy?
docs.text:
Question: “Is this model curated and published?”

python examples/small/03_trust_score.py

docs.title: Task 4 — Let AI search for you
docs.text:
Question: “Find MAPK models for me.”

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/01_find_models.py

docs.title: Task 5 — Full discovery study
docs.text:
Question: “Which model should I use for MAPK signalling?”

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/03_discovery_study.py

docs.title: Task 6 — Glycolysis demo (agent)
docs.text:
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py

docs.title: Task 7 — Workflow file (multi-step team)
docs.text:
No Python — uses PraisonAI workflow YAML:

praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

Agents work in sequence: find → summarise → simulate.

docs.title: Task 8 — Reproducibility bundle
docs.text:
python -c "
import praisonai_bio
from praisonai_bio.tools.repro_export import repro_export
print(repro_export.run(model_id='BIOMD0000000206', output_dir='/tmp/glycolysis_bundle'))
"

Creates model.sbml, metadata, and commands.sh in the folder.

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text:
Packaged workflows under recipes/bio/ — same automation via PraisonAI recipe commands.

Recipe Workflow biomodels-discovery Discovery pipeline → shortlist biomodels-research Full research workflow biomodels-simulate Baseline simulation biomodels-perturb Perturbation study biomodels-report Comparison report

docs.title: Run
docs.text:
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

Or point PraisonAI at the recipe folder:

export PRAISONAI_RECIPE_PATH=recipes/bio

Recipe files live in each subfolder as recipe.yaml.

docs.title: Cookbooks
docs.text:
Ready-made demos in workflows/cookbooks/:

praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/mapk_p53_discovery.yaml

docs.title: Tools at a glance
docs.text: What each tool does — no code required to understand.

docs.title: Discovery (safe, read-only)
docs.text: Tool You ask… You get… search_models “Find p53 models” List from BioModels.org get_modelinfo “Details for BIOMD…” Name, authors, description rank_models “Best fit for my question?” Ranked list with scores trust_scorecard “Is this curated?” Trust score + reasons compare_models “How do A and B differ?” Side-by-side metadata sbml_summarise “What’s in the model?” Species, reactions summary get_annotation “What databases link to this?” MIRIAM annotations query_article “Which paper?” DOI / PubMed links

docs.title: Simulation (needs pip install praisonai-bio[simulation])
docs.text: Tool You ask… You get… load_biomodel “Load this ID” SBML on disk simulate_model “Run for 100 time units” Time-course preview steady_state “Steady state?” Concentrations parameter_scan “Scan parameter X” Scan table preview_outcomes “Quick preview” Short simulation simulate_perturbation “Change one parameter” Perturbed run custom_plotter “Plot results” PNG file

docs.title: Reporting & reproducibility
docs.text: Tool You ask… You get… compare_simulations “Baseline vs perturbed?” Comparison summary save_model “Save SBML” File on disk repro_export “Repro bundle” Folder with SBML + checksums ask_question “Explain this model” Metadata for the agent to reason over

docs.title: Toolsets (bundles)
docs.text:
Toolset Who it's for biomodels-readonly Discovery only — safe sysbio-core Discovery + basic simulation simulation Running models sysbio-orchestrator Multi-step discovery agent

Use in agents: toolsets=["biomodels-readonly"]

Always run import praisonai_bio first.

docs.title: Toolsets
docs.text:
Toolsets are named bundles of tools for agents and YAML workflows.

Important

Run python -c "import praisonai_bio" before using toolsets in YAML or CLI.

docs.title: Toolsets
docs.text: Toolset Use when biomodels-readonly Search and metadata only (safe) sbml_analysis Load and summarise SBML sysbio-core Discovery + basic simulation simulation Run models (needs BASICO) reporting Compare and report sysbio-full All 21 tools sysbio-orchestrator Multi-step discovery agent sysbio-safe Read-only, no writes or simulation repro Reproducibility bundles

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
import praisonai_bio
from praisonaiagents import Agent

agent = Agent(
    name="scout",
    toolsets=["biomodels-readonly"],
    llm="gpt-4o-mini",
)
agent.start("Find curated MAPK models")

docs.title: YAML
docs.text:
agents:
  scout:
    role: BioModels Scout
    toolsets:
      - biomodels-readonly

docs.title: Tools in sysbio-full
docs.text:
T2B (11): search_models, get_modelinfo, load_biomodel, simulate_model, ask_question, steady_state, parameter_scan, custom_plotter, get_annotation, query_article, save_model

Discovery: rank_models, trust_scorecard, compare_models, preview_outcomes, sbml_summarise, sbml_validate, sedml_parse, simulate_perturbation, compare_simulations, repro_export

Details: Tools

docs.title: MCP servers
docs.text: Expose PraisonAIBio tools to Cursor, Claude Desktop, and other MCP clients.

docs.title: Servers
docs.text: Server Path Scope sysbio mcp/sysbio-server/server.py All 11 T2B tools biomodels mcp/biomodels-server/server.py Read-only search/metadata Package praisonai-bio-mcp Same as sysbio (entry point)

docs.title: Run locally
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python mcp/sysbio-server/server.py

Read-only (no BASICO):

python mcp/biomodels-server/server.py

docs.title: PraisonAI integration
docs.text:
praisonai mcp add   # register server in config
praisonai serve mcp

Workflow stub: workflows/platform/mcp_sysbio_server.yaml

docs.title: Resources (scaffold)
docs.text:
• biomodel://{id} — BioModels model
  • demo://glycolysis — BIOMD0000000206 bundle

See mcp/sysbio-server/resources.md in the repo.

docs.title: PraisonAIBio vs ClawBio
docs.text:
Capability ClawBio PraisonAIBio Domain Genomics / clinical bioinformatics Systems biology / SBML BioModels.org No Core ODE simulation No BASICO/COPASI Multi-agent workflows External LLM Native PraisonAI

PraisonAIBio complements ClawBio — v2 mcp/clawbio-bridge/ connects genomics context to pathway simulation.

docs.title: PraisonAIBio vs Talk2BioModels
docs.text:
Talk2BioModels (T2B) provides 11 simulation-focused tools after a model is chosen.

PraisonAIBio adds:

  • Automated discovery and ranking on BioModels.org
  • Multi-agent YAML workflows (Route, Parallel, Loop, HITL)
  • Trust scorecards and assumption review
  • CLI / YAML / Python parity via PraisonAI

All 11 T2B tools are replicated as PraisonAI @tool functions with entry-point registration.

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text: Multi-agent YAML workflows — no Python required.

docs.title: Discovery (workflows/discovery/)
docs.text:
File What it does biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml Question → ranked shortlist biomodels_assumption_review.yaml Assumption review + human approval biomodels_baseline_simulation.yaml Baseline simulation biomodels_perturbation_study.yaml Perturbation scenarios biomodels_comparison_report.yaml Comparison report biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml All phases (7 steps) 
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

docs.title: Cookbooks (workflows/cookbooks/)
docs.text: File Demo glycolysis_demo.yaml BIOMD0000000206 end-to-end mapk_p53_discovery.yaml MAPK / p53 shortlist

docs.title: Orchestration patterns
docs.text: File Pattern sysbio_route_by_input.yaml Route by domain parallel_sensitivity_scan.yaml Parallel scans team_model_review.yaml AgentTeam review full_platform_showcase.yaml Route + Parallel + Memory

docs.title: Flow (full research)
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Intake] --> B[Search BioModels]
    B --> C[Assumption review]
    C --> D[Simulate]
    D --> E[Report]

Before running: python -c "import praisonai_bio"

See For researchers and Recipes.
